channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: DoorDash is stepping up driver ID checks. Here’s why
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Zimbabwe Hosts World-Famous Robot Sophia for Innovation Fair
item.description: Sophia, the world-famous robot, visits Zimbabwe for an innovation fair, engaging with locals on climate change, substance abuse, and more.
item.dc:creator: By Farai Mutsaka, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 18 indispensable Android travel apps
item.description: For all the ways travel’s evolved over the years, one thing has remained maddeningly steadfast: Moving from one place to another is almost always a hassle. There’s endless room for inconvenience and error, and a journey rarely goes according to plan.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft .NET Community Toolkit backs partial properties
item.description: Microsoft has released .NET Community Toolkit 8.4, with support for partial properties in MVVM Toolkit source generators as well as new MVVM Toolkit diagnostic analyzers. The update was unveiled December 12. Source code for the .NET Community Toolkit can be found on GitHub.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Mysterious Death of the World's Rarest Whale: Scientists Uncover Clues to Its Demise
item.description: Scientists suspect head injuries caused the death of a spade-toothed whale, the world's rarest whale, after a thorough examination in New Zealand.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Addressing tech skills shortages through Australia’s correctional facilities
item.description: Australian CIOs are being called to support a groundbreaking initiative that is transforming lives and addressing critical talent shortages in the technology sector.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: San Jose, SJSU announce collaboration with NVIDIA to further workforce development, AI innovation
item.description: Nvidia signed two memorandums of understanding on Thursday -- including he first workforce pipeline initiative in the company’s history with a city government -- that offer training programs to both entities to upskill the workforce and drive innovation in San Jose.
item.dc:creator: Devan Patel, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft: No support or updates for Windows 11 PCs without minimum hardware requirements
item.description: Microsoft has offered a miniscule concession to users determined to install Windows 11 on PCs that don’t meet its minimum hardware requirements: you will be able to do it, but on your own head be it should things go wrong.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to keep your holiday shopping a secret on Amazon
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to keep your online Amazon gift purchases a secret from loved ones or friends this holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Lawmaker Warns of Chinese Drones Spying on US Soil
item.description: Congressman Raja Krishnamoorthi claims China's use of drones against the US is a 'nontrivial chance'
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: AWS adds Graviton Savings Dashboard to help enterprises optimize infra costs
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has launched a dashboard for its Arm-based Graviton CPU that will measure savings opportunities in an effort to help enterprises further optimize their expenditure on subscribed infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google launches Gemini 2.0 AI models, and showcases their powers in new agents
item.description: Google on Wednesday gave the public and developers a taste of the second generation of its Gemini frontier models, and a preview of some of the agents it will power.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: DoD sunsets Task Force Lima, introduces AI rapid capabilities cell
item.description: [Task Force Lima] had made so much progress and had such concrete recommendations that we moved to stand up this cell for actual execution,” said Radha Plumb. The post DoD sunsets Task Force Lima, introduces AI rapid capabilities cell first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Mozilla is removing Firefox’s anti-tracking function
item.description: Mozilla has announced that Do Not Track, a feature designed to prevent user tracking online, will be removed with version 135 of the company’s Firefox browser. The decision comes as more and more websites choose to ignore Do Not Track, leaving users with a false sense of security.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: EPA awards $144 million to California to phase out big diesel trucks
item.description: The money will go to the state's transportation department, cities and school districts to buy 480 zero-emission vehicles.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Google claims breakthrough with Willow quantum computing chip but no real-world use yet
item.description: Google’s announcement on Monday of its new quantum chip, called Willow, was full of eye-catching statements. For starters, Willow took less than a minute to perform a benchmark computation that would take one of today’s fastest supercomputers 10 septillion years to do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The makers and takers of WordPress
item.description: The idea of open-source software seems kind of nuts. Millions (billions?) of lines of code doing all kinds of amazing things and available for free? That sounds too good to be true. But it is true.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 3 takeaways from the Ultralytics AI Python library hack
item.description: When attackers compromised Ultralytics YOLO, a popular real-time object detection machine-learning package for Python, most assumed the Python Package Index, or PyPI, must be the point of failure. That made sense because the tampered software artifact was first found on PyPI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to delete multiple emails at once on iPhone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson shows iPhone users how to easily delete multiple emails and manage their inboxes to reduce digital clutter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Quick fixes to stop your Windows PC from crashing
item.description: If your Windows PC or laptop is constantly crashing, Kurt the CyberGuy has some easy fixes you can do at home to keep your computer running properly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
